# Encountered Issues — Recurring Bug Pattern Catalog

> **Auto-generated. Do not hand-edit.** To add a pattern, label the
> originating issue `recurring-pattern` and run `rite --refresh-encountered-issues`.

This catalog lists bug classes that recurred 2+ times during Sharkrite
dogfooding. Its purpose: let future Claude sessions recognize a familiar
pattern quickly and apply the known fix rather than re-diagnosing from scratch.

Each entry includes: the pattern name, recurrence instances (PRs + dates),
root-cause class, and the mitigation or lint rule that prevents recurrence.

## Sweep + lint rule for 'local' outside function (recurrence of #11 — claude-workflow.sh blocked all worktree creation)

**Issue:** [#77](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/issues/77)

**Fixed by:**

- PR [#81](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/pull/81)

**Closed:** 2026-05-31

**Description:**

`claude-workflow.sh:1364` had `local base_ref="origin/main"` in main script body (not inside a function). Under `set -u` (inherited from caller), `local` outside a function crashes with "local: can only be used in a function." This blocked all new-worktree creation during the 2026-05-31 testing batch — every issue that needed a fresh worktree (#31, #32, #33) failed within seconds.

Same bug class as milestone #11 (which fixed a similar instance in `local-review.sh:295`). The recurrence pattern indicates lib/core/ is still vulnerable; need a lint rule and a sweep to prevent further instances.

---

## Sweep codebase for set -e + pipefail silent-death from $(... | grep ...)

**Issue:** [#92](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/issues/92)

**Fixed by:**

- PR [#102](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/pull/102)

**Closed:** 2026-05-31

**Description:**

The 2026-05-31 #34 silent-death failure was one instance of a broader pattern: `set -euo pipefail` + a pipeline inside `$()` that ends in `grep` (or a similar command that exits 1 on no-match) silently kills the script when the grep finds nothing. The emergency fix added `|| true` to that ONE line; this issue tracks the codebase-wide sweep + a lint rule that prevents recurrence.

Risk: every unfixed instance is a latent silent-death bug waiting for an input that doesn't match the grep. Each such failure is debugging hell because the script just stops with no error output.

---

## Batch reporter doesn't count parent-deferred issues in Skipped tally

**Issue:** [#138](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/issues/138)

**Fixed by:**

- PR [#158](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/pull/158)

**Closed:** 2026-06-01

**Description:**

Batch reporter under-counts skipped issues when the skip reason is `waiting_for_parent` (parent PR deferral) — and likely other skip reasons that don't print the "skipping" banner. Users see `Total: 5, Processed: 4, Skipped: 0` and reasonably conclude 1 issue silently vanished. In reality the issue was deferred but the reporter forgot to add it to the Skipped count.

---

## Dev session deletes unrelated regression tests — scope boundary not enforced

**Issue:** [#157](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/issues/157)

**Fixed by:**

- PR [#189](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/pull/189)

**Closed:** 2026-06-01

**Description:**

Claude's dev session for issue #49 deleted `tests/regression/empty-diff-after-fetch.bats` even though the issue's scope was limited to regex tweaks. The PR merged this deletion silently because:
1. The assessment found 0 CRITICAL findings
2. The follow-up issue creation failed (sibling bug — filed separately)
3. The merge proceeded anyway

This is a **scope-boundary failure** — Claude doesn't reliably respect "DO NOT touch X" instructions when X happens to overlap with files it's editing. The lint rule changes touch test infrastructure indirectly; Claude appears to have decided the test was obsolete and removed it.

Pattern: every issue's body has a "Scope Boundary" section listing DO and DO NOT bullets. Sharkrite's prompt should explicitly enforce this — checking the actual diff against the scope boundary and refusing to commit changes that violate DO NOT.

---

## Sweep + lint + bats for lib file re-source safety (root cause of #61, #69, 93c7ddd, 2267841)

**Issue:** [#164](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/issues/164)

**Fixed by:**

- PR [#229](https://github.com/lifeunsubscribe/Sharkrite/pull/229)

**Closed:** 2026-06-02

**Description:**

Add a comprehensive sweep + lint rule + bats test for re-source safety. The goal: every file in `lib/` MUST be safe to source multiple times AND must work correctly whether sourced from a parent that already loaded dependencies OR from a fresh subprocess that loaded nothing yet. The recent history of patches (#61, #69, `93c7ddd`, `2267841`) is a sample of the class — without enforcement, more variants will keep surfacing.

---
